remember: Students will be able to recall the role of microbes in human welfare.
Students will be able to list the various applications of microbes in medicine, agriculture, and industry.
Students will be able to describe the production of antibiotics and vaccines by microbes.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of probiotics and their benefits.
Students will be able to understand the role of microbes in food and beverages.
Students will be able to describe the use of microbes in biofertilizers and biogas production.
apply: Students will be able to apply their knowledge of microbes to develop new products and technologies.
Students will be able to design experiments to study the role of microbes in human welfare.
Students will be able to evaluate the benefits and limitations of using microbes in different applications.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the impact of microbes on human health and the environment.
Students will be able to compare the advantages and disadvantages of different microbes used in various applications.
Students will be able to evaluate the economic and social benefits of using microbes in human welfare.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: To understand the importance of microbes in human welfare.
To describe the role of microbes in medicine, agriculture, and industry.
To explain the concept of probiotics and their benefits.
board_exam_objectives: To describe the applications of microbes in medicine (antibiotics and vaccines).
To explain the role of microbes in food and beverages (fermentation).
To describe the use of microbes in biofertilizers and biogas production (agriculture).
